response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has genuine strengths in distributed systems and engineering craftsmanship, but critically fails to meet the most fundamental requirement: 8 years of C++ experience. C++ is absent from his skills section entirely, with only a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments involving C/C++ components and a bachelor thesis project. The role's core technical stack is a near-total mismatch — his career has been built almost exclusively on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is also no evidence of cloud compute platform ownership, technical leadership of infrastructure teams, or the kind of large-scale compute/storage architecture work this role centers on.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby, building low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong engineering culture advocate with consistent application of TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development across multiple roles and organizations
Broad polyglot background and platform range (web, mobile, embedded, GIS) showing genuine adaptability and systems-level thinking
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient — the role requires 8 years minimum, but C++ does not appear in the skills section and is only marginally referenced in one short role and a thesis project
No cloud compute platform technical leadership — AWS CDK usage at Tencent is peripheral tooling, not ownership of large-scale cloud infrastructure architecture
No formal technical lead or principal engineer experience in cloud, DevOps, or infrastructure domains; staff-level titles held but strategic platform ownership is not evidenced
